    An update to SettingsProvider inside a test was not wrapped in act(...).
    
    When testing, code that causes React state updates should be wrapped into act(...):
    
    act(() => {
      /* fire events that update state */
    });
    /* assert on the output */
    
    This ensures that you're testing the behavior the user would see in the browser. Learn more at https://react.dev/link/wrap-tests-with-act

      ΓêÜ should render double zero correctly (7 ms)
      ΓêÜ should render chip when bet amount is greater than 0 (12 ms)
      ΓêÜ should render chip with amount 0 when no bet (7 ms)
    interactions
      ΓêÜ should call onNumberPress when pressed (16 ms)
      ΓêÜ should call onBetAreaPress when pressed (57 ms)
      ΓêÜ should handle press without onBetAreaPress callback (7 ms)
    accessibility
      ΓêÜ should have correct accessibility label for red number without bet (7 ms)
      ΓêÜ should have correct accessibility label for black number without bet (19 ms)
      ΓêÜ should have correct accessibility label for green number (zero) (8 ms)
      ΓêÜ should include bet amount in accessibility label when bet is placed (7 ms)
      ΓêÜ should have button accessibility role (7 ms)
      ΓêÜ should have accessibility hint for placing bets (14 ms)
    memoization
      ΓêÜ should not re-render when same props are passed (9 ms)
      ΓêÜ should re-render when number changes (8 ms)
      ΓêÜ should re-render when bet amount changes (8 ms)
    different cell sizes
      ΓêÜ should render with small cell size (6 ms)
      ΓêÜ should render with large cell size (9 ms)
      ΓêÜ should calculate chip size based on cell size (12 ms)

    react-test-renderer is deprecated. See https://react.dev/warnings/react-test-renderer

    An update to SettingsProvider inside a test was not wrapped in act(...).
    
    When testing, code that causes React state updates should be wrapped into act(...):
    
    act(() => {
      /* fire events that update state */
    });
    /* assert on the output */
    
    This ensures that you're testing the behavior the user would see in the browser. Learn more at https://react.dev/link/wrap-tests-with-act
